name: Alto la Torneria and Alto del Torno – circular from Llanes
name_original: Alto la Torneria y Alto del Torno – circular desde Llanes
region_intro: Road cycling around Llanes, located on Spain's "Green Coast" in Asturias, offers diverse terrain where the Cantabrian Sea meets the foothills of the Picos de Europa. The region features a dramatic coastline with rugged cliffs and numerous beaches, contrasting with the rising inland terrain of the Sierra del Cuera limestone range. This geography provides varied elevation profiles, from coastal paths to challenging mountain ascents, often with panoramic views of both the sea and mountains. Best road cycling routes around Llanes The most popular road cycling route is Alto la Torneria and Alto del Torno – circular from Llanes, a 35.3 miles (56.8 km) trail that takes 3 hours 1 minute to complete. This route features significant climbs into the Picos de Europa foothills with green scenery. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Queen's Viewpoint – Lagos de Covadonga loop from Posada, a difficult 60.5 miles (97.4 km) path. This route includes the challenging ascent to Lagos de Covadonga, offering extensive mountain views. Local road cyclists also love the Arenas de Cabrales – Alto de Ortiguero Descent loop from Posada, a 56.0 miles (90.1 km) trail leading through the mountainous terrain near Arenas de Cabrales, often completed in about 4 hours 21 minutes. Road cycling around Llanes is defined by dramatic coastlines, the Sierra del Cuera limestone range, and the Picos de Europa foothills. The network offers options for various ability levels, from moderate coastal rides to difficult mountain climbs. The routes in Llanes are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.8 stars from over 60 reviews. More than 3000 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Llanes's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many road cycling routes are available in Llanes?
A: Llanes offers a diverse network of over 60 road cycling routes. These routes cater to various skill levels, ranging from moderate coastal rides to challenging mountain climbs, ensuring there's something for every road cyclist.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect when road cycling around Llanes?
A: Road cycling in Llanes features a unique blend of dramatic coastal landscapes and the foothills of the Picos de Europa. You'll encounter routes along the rugged Cantabrian Sea coastline, as well as ascents into the Sierra del Cuera limestone range, offering varied elevation profiles and panoramic views of both the sea and mountains.

Q: Are there road cycling routes suitable for beginners or those looking for an easier ride in Llanes?
A: While many routes in Llanes involve significant climbs, there are options for less experienced riders. The region's coastal path, for example, offers a scenic ride with minimal gradient, ideal for enjoying the landscapes. For a moderate challenge, consider routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14993408" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Playa de Poo – San Antolín de Bedón Beach loop from Llanes&lt;/a&gt;, which covers about 55 km with moderate elevation.

Q: Where can I find challenging road cycling routes in the Llanes area?
A: For experienced cyclists seeking a challenge, Llanes provides demanding mountain ascents. Routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38171308" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Queen's Viewpoint – Lagos de Covadonga loop from Posada&lt;/a&gt; offer extensive mountain views and significant climbs, including the famous ascent to Lagos de Covadonga. Another challenging option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1070225105" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alto la Torneria and Alto del Torno – circular from Llanes&lt;/a&gt;, which features substantial climbs into the Picos de Europa foothills.

Q: What are some scenic viewpoints or landmarks I can see along road cycling routes in Llanes?
A: Llanes is rich in natural beauty and viewpoints. Many routes offer views of the dramatic coastline, rugged cliffs, and the distant Picos de Europa. Specific highlights include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5182159" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alto de la Tornería Scenic Road&lt;/a&gt;, offering green scenery and fantastic views. You might also encounter the impressive 'bufones' (blowholes) along the coast, such as the Bufones de Pría, which create dramatic jets of seawater during strong waves.

Q: Are there any circular road cycling routes starting from Llanes?
A: Yes, many road cycling routes in Llanes are designed as circular loops, allowing you to start and finish in the same location. A popular example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1070225105" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alto la Torneria and Alto del Torno – circular from Llanes&lt;/a&gt;, which provides a challenging loop through the Picos de Europa foothills.

Q: What is the best time of year to go road cycling in Llanes?
A: Llanes benefits from its coastal location, offering pleasant conditions for road cycling for much of the year. Spring and autumn generally provide mild temperatures and less crowded roads, making them ideal. Summer can also be excellent, though some routes might be busier, especially near popular beaches.

Q: Can I access road cycling routes in Llanes using public transport?
A: Llanes and its surrounding towns, such as Posada, are generally well-connected by regional public transport, including bus services. While specific bike carriage policies vary, it's often possible to reach starting points for routes, particularly those originating from larger towns. It's advisable to check with local transport providers for their current regulations regarding bicycles.

Q: Are there family-friendly road cycling options in Llanes?
A: For families, the coastal path offers a relatively flat and scenic option to enjoy the stunning beaches and coves of Llanes. While many of the listed road cycling routes involve significant elevation, the region's greenways and less trafficked local roads can provide safer and more enjoyable experiences for families with children, allowing them to explore the beautiful landscapes at a gentler pace.

Q: What do other cyclists say about road cycling in Llanes?
A: The road cycling routes around Llanes are highly regarded by the komoot community, with an impressive average rating of 4.8 stars from over 60 reviews. Cyclists frequently praise the diverse terrain, from challenging mountain climbs with rewarding views to scenic coastal rides, and the generally good condition of the roads.

Q: Are there any specific mountain passes or climbs popular with road cyclists near Llanes?
A: Yes, the region around Llanes is known for its rewarding mountain passes and climbs. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5182159" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alto de la Tornería Scenic Road&lt;/a&gt; is a popular ascent into the Picos de Europa foothills, offering green scenery and fantastic views. Other notable passes include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5149150" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alto de Ortiguero Descent&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/7610374" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alto El Mazucu&lt;/a&gt;, which are part of more challenging routes.

Q: What are the typical distances and elevations for road cycling routes in Llanes?
A: Road cycling routes in Llanes vary significantly in distance and elevation. You can find routes ranging from moderate rides of around 50-60 km with several hundred meters of elevation gain, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20506591" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ribadesella Town Center – San Antolín de Bedón Beach loop from Llanes&lt;/a&gt; (58.6 km, 499m elevation), to difficult routes exceeding 90 km with over 2000 meters of climbing, like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38171308" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Queen's Viewpoint – Lagos de Covadonga loop from Posada&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there coastal road cycling routes in Llanes?
A: Absolutely. Llanes is famous for its dramatic coastline, and many road cycling routes incorporate stunning coastal sections. You can enjoy rides that hug the Cantabrian Sea, passing by over 30 idyllic beaches and coves, characterized by white sand and rugged cliffs. These routes often offer breathtaking views of the ocean and the distant mountains.
